key_topics: Number: integers, powers, roots; HCF and LCM using prime factorisation
Fractions, decimals, percentages: advanced calculations
Ratio, proportion, and rates of change
Algebra: expanding brackets, factorising, simplifying expressions
Equations: solving linear equations and inequalities
Sequences: arithmetic and geometric sequences, nth term
Functions and graphs: coordinates, straight line graphs, y = mx + c
Geometry: angles in polygons, properties of parallel lines
Pythagoras' theorem: finding missing sides
Area and perimeter: circles, composite shapes
Surface area and volume: prisms, cylinders
Probability: experimental and theoretical, tree diagrams
Statistics: grouped data, frequency polygons, averages from tables
competences: Apply Pythagoras' theorem to right-angled triangles
Plot and interpret straight line graphs using y = mx + c
Find averages from grouped frequency tables
typical_mistakes: Squaring a sum incorrectly (a+b)² ≠ a² + b²
Confusing gradient (m) and y-intercept (c) in y = mx + c
Forgetting to square-root the final answer in Pythagoras
Errors in expanding double brackets (FOIL method)

key_topics: Reading: analytical response to unseen texts (literature and non-fiction)
Exploring how writers create effects through language and structure
Comparing two texts: similar themes, different techniques
Writing: narrative with controlled narrative voice
Analytical writing: structured response to texts with PEE (Point, Evidence, Explain)
Persuasive and discursive writing: rhetorical devices
Research project: extended writing with citation
Grammar: advanced punctuation, complex syntax
Vocabulary in context: connotation, denotation, field-specific language
Speaking: debate, formal discussion, verbal presentation with slides
competences: Analyse writer's craft with structured PEE responses
Compare two texts for similarities and differences
Write controlled, technically accurate narrative and discursive texts
Use rhetorical devices effectively in spoken and written argument
typical_mistakes: PEE responses that don't explain the effect clearly
Comparisons that list differences without analysing them
Over-quoting (too long quotes) without sufficient analysis

key_topics: Biology: photosynthesis and respiration — inputs, outputs, conditions
Biology: human reproduction, puberty, development
Biology: ecosystems — nutrient cycling, energy flow
Chemistry: particle theory — explaining properties of states of matter
Chemistry: elements, compounds, mixtures — separation techniques
Chemistry: chemical reactions — word equations, conservation of mass
Physics: speed, distance, time — calculations
Physics: forces — resultant forces, pressure
Physics: energy — stores and transfers
competences: Write and interpret word equations for chemical reactions
Calculate speed using the speed-distance-time triangle
Explain photosynthesis and respiration in terms of reactants and products
Describe particle behaviour in different states and during changes of state
typical_mistakes: Errors in speed-distance-time calculations (using wrong formula)
Thinking mixtures and compounds are the same
Confusing reactants and products in chemical equations
